rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN YEE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/20/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/18/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) If the petitioner intends to request fees for his services as conservator during the same period, a separate fee petition must be filed in the related conservatorship case to be reviewed and adjudicated with this trust accounting petition. 3) Notice must be given to the Department of State Hospitals. 4) The address of the Department of Health Care Services, Special Needs Trust Unit, listed on the proof of service appears incorrect. 4) The total amount of the trustee fees (paragraph 9 & Exhibit B of petition) appears incorrect. 5) Legal costs paid on 3/2/22 & 12/21/22 (Schedule B) must be itemized. 6) Exhibit C shows a number of hours billed by multiple attorneys and paralegals for their internal communications within the firm. Is it reasonable to charge the trust for all of those communications?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MIKHAIL M. VAYNBERG AND BEATRISA VAYNBERG LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee, For Accounting, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty, And Surcharge
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 1/24/2024 at 4:45 pm* As the parties requested, the matter is continued to 3/12/2024 at the judicial officer's permission. The following procedural issues to be cleared: 1. Petition prays transfer of assets to Trust B and damages under sec. 859. Appears the petition includes the claim under PC. sec. 850. Notice of Hearing On Petition To Determine Claim to Property must be served pursuant to sec. 851, especially on Yafim Ratinski (the trustee of Trust A) and Minna Ferna (the temp trustee of Trust B). 2. Notice must be served on the successor-in-interest of Mikhail Vaynberg who is/was the beneficiary. Notice on file showed the service was made on his attorney in this proceeding. Petition may need to address whether there is any personal representative was appointed in Israel or in the US, whichever applicable. 3. Caption needs to reflect all relief being sought. CRC 7.102. Then, the notice needs to sufficiently describe the caption. CRC 7.50.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEATO OSIMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/13/2024. The following issue must be addressed (e-mailed to counsel on 01/10/2024): Did the petitioner close the decedent's Citibank account as administrator of the estate and collect the funds? If so, the funds collected by the petitioner must be accounted for as an asset of the estate. A new petition for final distribution must be filed with a final account* (or a final report on waiver of account). *Permission to exceed the two-year account period must be sought. See LRSF 14.34(A). Note: Unlike an order for final distrbution, no omnibus clause can be included in an order terminating the estate for lack of assets subject to administration.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZHAOHONG YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/20/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/19/2023): 1) Item 1 of the DE-120 form must state the complete title of the petition including the request for the attorney's compensation. CRC 7.50. 2) The decedent's mother's first name was spelled "Mei" or "May". Please confirm the correct spelling. 3) The assets on hand are exactly the same as the bank accounts listed on the corrected inventory and appraisal. Did the petitioner not open an interest-bearing bank account in the estate's name, as required by Probate Code section 9652? 4) The calculation of net cash available for final distribution is incorrect, unless the balance of the account 6066 is $213,606.28, the same amount as the corrected inventory and appraisal value. 5) The bank accounts identified by the decedent's mother in her disclaimer are not listed on the corrected inventory and appraisal and thus are not part of the assets on hand. Please explain why the decedent's mother should not be the sole distributee from whom a waiver of account must be obtained. 6) The omnibus clause must be corrected to identify the decedent's mother as the sole distributee, as the scope of her disclaimer was limited to known bank accounts.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENYATTA O. SCOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/21/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/24/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 11/21/2023 and 01/02/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Re the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form: i) It must be dated and signed by a person other than the petitioner. See Probate Code section 1261 & CCP section 1013a. ii) Item 4a must specify the date of mailing. iii) Item 4b must specify the city and state from which required papers were mailed. 3) No proof of publication is on file. 4) Item 2a of the petition must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 5) Item 2b(3) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 6) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. 7) Item 3d: i) Please provide a brief description of personal property. ii) If the estimated value of personal property does not exceed $184,500, please explain why the heirs are not proceeding with the small estate affidavit nonprobate procedure under Probate Code sections 13100 et seq. 8) Item 3e(4) must be unchecked, as it does not apply to the petitioner. If all other heirs waived the petitioner's bond, item 3e(3) must be checked, and DE-142 forms (bond waivers) signed by the heirs (other than the petitioner) must be filed. 9) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 10) Item 3g(2)(c) must be checked, with the petitioner's relationship to the decedent specified. 11) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 13) Item 6b must be checked, if the decedent was survived by siblings. 14) Item 9: There were no attachments (4 pages) to the petition. 15) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. 16) A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate and describe such liabilities and claims. A minimum bond ($20,000) is required for a personal representative who does not reside in California, regardless of bond waivers and absence of any liabilities or claims. Note: An appearance may be required on 02/21/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, if no satisfactory progress is shown by papers filed to address the issues.
